SessionStart.name: detect-docker-context
SessionStart.description: Detect Docker environment on session start

hooks.prompt: Silently check if Docker-related files exist in the current directory (docker-compose*.yml, Dockerfile). If any are found, output a single brief status line: '[odoo-docker] Docker files detected. Use /odoo-docker for infrastructure commands.' If none found, say nothing.

PostToolUse.name: docker-compose-changed
PostToolUse.description: When docker-compose.yml is modified, suggest recreating containers

hooks.message:
docker-compose file was modified. Recreate containers to apply changes:

```bash
# Recreate with updated config
docker compose up -d

# If service definitions changed
docker compose down && docker compose up -d

# If Dockerfile changed
docker compose up -d --build
```

PostToolUse.name: dockerfile-changed
PostToolUse.description: When Dockerfile is modified, suggest rebuilding the image

hooks.message:
Dockerfile was modified. Rebuild the image to apply changes:

```bash
# Rebuild and restart
docker compose build && docker compose up -d

# Clean rebuild (no cache)
docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d
```

PostToolUse.name: nginx-conf-changed
PostToolUse.description: When nginx.conf is modified, suggest reloading nginx

hooks.message:
nginx.conf was modified. Reload nginx to apply:

```bash
# Test config first
docker compose exec nginx nginx -t

# Reload (no downtime)
docker compose exec nginx nginx -s reload

# Or restart nginx container
docker compose restart nginx
```

Reminder: ensure `proxy_mode = True` in odoo.conf when behind nginx.

PostToolUse.name: env-file-changed
PostToolUse.description: When .env is modified, suggest container restart

hooks.message:
.env file was modified. Recreate containers to apply new environment variables:

```bash
docker compose down && docker compose up -d
```

Note: `docker compose restart` does NOT pick up .env changes. You must `down` then `up`.

PostToolUse.name: entrypoint-changed
PostToolUse.description: When entrypoint.sh is modified, remind about chmod +x and rebuild

hooks.message:
entrypoint.sh was modified. Ensure it's executable and rebuild:

```bash
chmod +x entrypoint.sh
docker compose build && docker compose up -d
```

If the entrypoint is volume-mounted (not COPY'd), a container restart is sufficient.

PostToolUse.name: requirements-changed
PostToolUse.description: When requirements.txt is modified, suggest rebuilding the Docker image

hooks.message:
requirements.txt was modified. Rebuild the Docker image to install new dependencies:

```bash
docker compose build && docker compose up -d

# Clean rebuild if cached layers cause issues
docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d
```

Note: `docker compose restart` does NOT install new Python packages. You must rebuild the image.

PostToolUse.name: odoo-conf-docker
PostToolUse.description: When an Odoo .conf file is modified, suggest container restart. Broad path (**/conf/*.conf) is intentional — the suggestion is harmless in non-Docker contexts and avoids missing Docker configs in non-standard locations.

hooks.message:
Odoo configuration file was modified. Restart the Odoo container:

```bash
docker compose restart odoo
```

Key settings to verify:
- `data_dir = /var/lib/odoo` (CRITICAL for filestore)
- `workers >= 2` (production)
- `proxy_mode = True` (when behind nginx)
- `gevent_port = 8072` (v17+) or `longpolling_port = 8072` (v14-16)
